info.title: Perfex CRM REST API
info.description: Comprehensive REST API for Perfex CRM system with full CRUD operations for all entities. Supports global pagination with ?page=X&per_page=Y parameters.

get.summary: List all Contacts of a Customer (or a single contact when id is provided)

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Contact unique ID (provide to get single contact)

404.description: Invoice Add Fail or validation errors

409.description: Conflict - Invoice number already in use

409.description: Conflict - validation errors or number already in use

parameters.description: Subscription unique ID. If not provided, returns all subscriptions

get.summary: Get all records from a custom table
get.description: Retrieves all records from the specified custom table. The table name should be the exact name as it exists in the database (no prefix will be added).

parameters.name: table_name
parameters.description: Name of the custom database table (exact table name as it exists in the database)

200.description: Array of records with all columns and values

post.summary: Insert a new record into a custom table
post.description: Inserts a new record into the specified custom table. All columns in the request body must exist in the table, otherwise an error will be returned.

parameters.name: table_name
parameters.description: Name of the custom database table (exact table name as it exists in the database)

requestBody.description: Record data. All keys must correspond to existing columns in the table.

400.description: Bad request - Invalid data or column does not exist

message.example: Column 'invalid_column' does not exist in table 'george'

get.summary: Get a specific record from a custom table by ID
get.description: Retrieves a specific record from the custom table by its ID.

parameters.name: table_name
parameters.description: Name of the custom database table (exact table name as it exists in the database)

200.description: Record with all columns and values

400.description: Table name or ID is required

404.description: Table or record does not exist

put.summary: Update a record in a custom table
put.description: Updates an existing record in the custom table. All columns in the request body must exist in the table, otherwise an error will be returned.

parameters.name: table_name
parameters.description: Name of the custom database table (exact table name as it exists in the database)

requestBody.description: Record data to update. All keys must correspond to existing columns in the table.

400.description: Bad request - Invalid data or column does not exist

message.example: Column 'invalid_column' does not exist in table 'george'

404.description: Table or record does not exist

delete.summary: Delete a record from a custom table
delete.description: Deletes a specific record from the custom table by its ID.

parameters.name: table_name
parameters.description: Name of the custom database table (exact table name as it exists in the database)

400.description: Table name or ID is required

404.description: Table or record does not exist

include_shipping.description: Set 'on' if you want add Shipping Address

cancel_overdue_reminders.description: Prevent sending overdue remainders for invoice

repeat_every_custom.description: If recurring is custom set number gap

repeat_type_custom.description: If recurring is custom set gap option (day/week/month/year)

subtotal.description: Sub Total (calculation based on item Qty, Rate and Tax) (required)

total.description: Total (calculation based on subtotal, Discount and Adjustment) (required)

admin.description: Staff ID (if provided, reply is from staff member)

status.description: Ticket status after reply (default: 1 for customer, 3 for staff)

repeat_type_custom.description: If recurring is custom set gap option (day/week/month/year)

invoiceid.description: Invoice ID associated with the payment (required)

paymentmode.description: Payment mode (required, e.g., cash, credit card, etc.)

description_in_item.description: Indicates if the description is included in the item (1 or 0)

in_test_environment.description: Indicates if the subscription is in a test environment (1 or 0)
